rationale: Russian strikes on Kyiv continue as background; Crimea panic amid Ukrainian attacks is a mild new signal for Black Sea security but no direct threat to Romania. Score adjusted down: ongoing situation without qualitative change.

top_events.title: 'Island surrounded by war': Crimeans panic amid Ukrainian attacks
top_events.summary: Ukrainian strikes on Crimea create new anxiety in the Black Sea region, which is geographically close to Romania, but no direct threat or escalation toward Romanian territory is confirmed.

top_events.title: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
top_events.summary: Continued Russian bombardment of Ukraine remains background noise for Romania with no direct spillover. Pattern is well established.

top_events.title: Oil prices fall and shares jump after US-Iran deal announced
top_events.summary: Continued oil price decline offers modest relief to Romanian consumers and the economy, but this is an ongoing trend already reflected in daily life.
